title: Numerical Computing, Arrays, Visualization, and Reproducibility · Quantity Clinic: Floating Point
summary: Implement and test reusable computational models over real datasets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int floating point increment in a tested numerical Python notebook and module. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain floating-point approximation and compare values with justified tolerances.
Evaluate whose costs and benefits an objective includes or omits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Floating Point evidence
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (5 × 8) − (1 × 2).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (5 × 8) − (1 × 2).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0 of the exact value.
